description: This plot summarizes the room-temperature tensile properties of Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels with varying Mn contents, with x-axis representing yield/tensile strength in MPa and y-axis representing total elongation in %. Steels with different phase constitutions (single austenite phase, single ferrite phase, ferrite-austenite-MC/κ, austenite-based duplex, ferrite-based duplex) are separated by different colored dashed envelopes. Mn contents are categorized into four ranges: 12~18 wt.% (red triangles), 18~25 wt.% (purple squares), 25~28 wt.% (green triangles), and 28~33 wt.% (orange hexagons). Three reference lines of product of strength and elongation (30, 50, 70 GPa·%) are also plotted. It is observed that single austenitic steels generally have the highest strength-elongation product, exceeding 70 GPa·% with maximum total elongation close to 100%, while ferrite-based duplex steels show higher strength but lower plasticity, with strength-elongation product mostly around 30 GPa·%. The core conclusion supported is that the tensile performance of Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels is strongly dependent on phase constitution and Mn content, and single austenitic steels achieve the optimal strength-plasticity synergy.
